description: Evoluir o editor com manipulacao direta no canvas, presets de tamanho e uma base priorizada de melhorias futuras.

userStories.title: Mover camada por arraste
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to drag a selected layer on the canvas so that I can reposition it without editing numeric fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a selected renderable layer updates its x and y values
The inspector reflects the new x and y values after drag
Dragging keeps the layer inside the canvas bounds or clamps it predictably
Selection remains active after the drag ends
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Camadas renderizaveis podem ser arrastadas no canvas; delta considera zoom, aplica clamp e atualiza inspector/persistencia no fim do gesto.

userStories.title: Redimensionar camada por alcas
userStories.description: As a creator, I want resize handles on the selected layer so that I can adjust width and height visually.
acceptanceCriteria: A selected renderable layer shows visible resize handles
Dragging a resize handle updates width and height values
Minimum width and height prevent invalid or invisible layers
The inspector reflects the new dimensions after resize
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Camada selecionada mostra oito alcas de resize. Drag nas alcas atualiza width/height com tamanho minimo e clamp no canvas.

userStories.title: Adicionar snap e nudges
userStories.description: As a creator, I want grid snap and keyboard nudges so that I can align layers with predictable precision.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow keys move the selected layer by a small fixed step
Shift plus arrow keys move the selected layer by a larger fixed step
When snap is enabled, drag and keyboard movement align to the configured grid size
The UI exposes whether snap is enabled
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Snap pode ser ativado/desativado na toolbar. Setas movem camada selecionada; Shift aumenta o passo.

userStories.title: Selecionar presets de canvas
userStories.description: As a creator, I want common canvas presets so that I can start from desktop, tablet, mobile, or custom dimensions.
acceptanceCriteria: The canvas toolbar or inspector exposes preset options for desktop, tablet, mobile and custom
Choosing a preset updates canvas width and height
Choosing custom preserves manually edited dimensions
Existing templates continue to load with their current canvas dimensions
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Presets desktop/tablet/mobile/square/story foram adicionados para novo template e canvas atual. Dimensoes customizadas ainda pendentes.

userStories.title: Persistir e exportar preset
userStories.description: As a creator, I want the selected canvas preset saved and exported so that the template keeps its intended format across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected preset persists after page reload through local storage
JSON export includes the canvas preset value
CSS export keeps using the saved canvas dimensions
Templates without an explicit preset still load with a safe fallback
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Preset fica em selectedTemplate.canvas.preset, persiste em localStorage e entra no JSON exportado. CSS continua usando dimensoes salvas.

userStories.title: Preparar backlog de melhorias futuras
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want future canvas improvements captured as small candidates so that the next planning round is easier to prioritize.
acceptanceCriteria: The implementation notes or product task file lists at least five future canvas improvements
Each future improvement is written as a small, independently testable candidate
The list includes undo or redo, smart guides, layer lock, multi-select grouping and JSON import
No source code changes are required for this story
Typecheck is not required unless code changes are introduced
userStories.notes: Backlog capturado no PRD e ROADMAP: dimensoes customizadas, undo/redo, smart guides, layer lock, multi-select/grouping e JSON import.
